CONTENT.Career: Kameri began his career at FC Liefering. Between 2016 and 2017 he played for the youth of the partner club FC Red Bull Salzburg for a short time before moving to the Red Bull Salzburg academy for the 2018/19 season. After three years at the academy, he moved to the professional squad of the farm team FC Liefering for the 2021/22 season.His debut for Liefering in the 2. Liga he gave in July 2021, when he was in the starting line-up on the first day of that season against Kapfenberger SV.On 8 February 2024, he joined Swiss Super League side Grasshopper Club Zürich on loan for the remainder of the season. After just six appearances, of which he started in two, he suffered an injury to his outer meniscus that would require surgery and ruled him out for the rest of the season. On 19 June 2024, his return to Salzburg was announced.
CONTENT.International career: Born in Austria, Kameri is of Kosovo-Albanian descent. He is a youth international for Austria.

SUMMARY: Dijon Kameri (born 20 April 2004) is an Austrian professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for Swiss Super League club Grasshoppers, on loan from Austrian Bundesliga club Red Bull Salzburg.
